original_title: Octahedral–Tetrahedral Site Synergism in Spinel Oxides Enables 1700 h‐Stable Electrosynthesis of 2,5‐Furandicarboxylic Acid in Near‐Neutral Media
title: An electrocatalyst for HMF oxidation that works in neutral water
blurb: A spinel oxide with mixed octahedral and tetrahedral sites catalyzes HMF oxidation to FDCA in neutral water instead of harsh alkaline conditions, reportedly stable for 1700 hours. Neutral conditions are genuinely better for HMF selectivity, though running this at scale with real electrochemical hardware is the practical question.

original_title: Image quality and lesion detectability in simulated [124I]MIBG cardiac imaging across 3-generation PET/CT scanners using a phantom model: Implications for activity and acquisition time reduction in clinical practice
title: Newer PET scanners might cut 124I-MIBG doses for cardiac imaging
blurb: Long axial field-of-view PET/CT scanners improve image quality and defect detection with 124I-MIBG compared to older systems, potentially reducing activity and scan time for cardiac sympathetic imaging. The gains are real on phantoms, though clinical translation in actual patients remains to be shown.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Implementation of a Multidisciplinary Radiopharmaceutical Dosimetry Program: Key Practical Considerations and Challenges.
title: Building a radiopharmaceutical dosimetry program from scratch
blurb: A practical review of considerations and obstacles when establishing a multidisciplinary dosimetry program for radiopharmaceuticals in clinical practice. The value is in lessons from running programs, though staffing, equipment, and regulatory requirements are highly institution-specific.

original_title: Thiols as Key HAT Catalysts in Photoredox Catalysis: Diversifying the Hydrofunctionalization Landscape of Unactivated Alkenes
title: Photoredox hydrofunctionalization of unactivated alkenes using thiols
blurb: Visible-light photocatalysis with thiols as hydrogen-atom-transfer catalysts can hydrofunctionalize unactivated alkenes, where photoredox chemistry typically stalls. It's a solid addition to the toolkit, though the substrate scope is limited and you'll likely need several approaches in practice.

original_title: Asymmetric Total Synthesis of (+)‐Ineleganolide
title: Total synthesis of a baroque pentacyclic diterpenoid
blurb: A 13-step total synthesis of ineleganolide, a highly oxidized cembrane norditerpenoid with a challenging pentacyclic cage. Total syntheses this elaborate are typically demonstrations of synthetic problem-solving rather than practical routes, but the tactics here might apply to related cembranoids.

original_title: Combined therapy with ALK inhibitors and a novel EGFR-targeting antibody‒drug conjugate as a strategy for ALK-rearranged non–small cell lung cancers
title: Dual targeting ALK and EGFR in resistant lung cancer
blurb: A combination therapy pairing ALK inhibitors with a novel EGFR-targeting ADC in ALK-rearranged NSCLC to overcome therapeutic resistance. Dual targeting makes sense mechanistically, though managing toxicity from two active drugs is the practical hurdle.

original_title: Adapting Bower's intramolecular aziridination reaction allows for a metal-free synthesis of N–H aziridines
title: Intermolecular aziridination drops the metal
blurb: Intermolecular N–H aziridination adapted from Bower's intramolecular version, running metal-free. Avoids the ligand overhead of metal catalysts, though how it stacks up on scope versus catalytic routes is another question.

original_title: Dearomative [2 + 2] photocycloaddition to difluoro bicyclo[2.1.1]hexane bioisosteres
title: Visible-light photocycloaddition builds bicyclic scaffolds from heteroaromatics
blurb: Visible-light [2+2] photocycloaddition dearomatizes heteroaromatic gem-difluoro alkenes into bicyclo[2.1.1]hexane scaffolds with broad scope and excellent diastereoselectivity. Mild, but only useful if that particular scaffold solves something for your drug.

original_title: Targeting Copper in Cancer: Chelators to Counteract Cuproplasia and Ionophores to Promote Cuproptosis
title: Copper as a cancer target
blurb: Medicinal chemistry approaches to targeting copper in cancer, using chelators to block cuproplasia or ionophores to promote cuproptosis. Whether either approach actually translates to clinical benefit is still open.

original_title: Sustainable Synthesis of Bio‐Based Furanic and Aromatic Amines Using an Optimized Whole‐Cell Transaminase–Decarboxylase Cascade in E. coli RARE
title: Biocatalytic cascade makes primary amine synthesis work
blurb: Transaminase coupled with pyruvate decarboxylase to bypass the unfavorable equilibrium that usually stops enzymatic amine synthesis. They're targeting bio-based furanic and aromatic precursors, so the green chemistry angle is real if precursor costs work out.

original_title: Nickel-Catalyzed Cross-Electrophile Couplings of Tetraarylphosphonium Salts with Aryl Bromides
title: Nickel couples phosphonium salts to aryl bromides
blurb: Nickel-catalyzed cross-electrophile coupling of tetraarylphosphonium salts with aryl bromides, avoiding the organometallic nucleophile step. Phosphonium salt reactivity isn't as predictable as standard partners, so substrate scope probably trails the headline.

original_title: Newman-Kwart Rearrangement Enabled by Flavin Photoredox Catalysis
title: Photoredox makes Newman-Kwart rearrangement mild
blurb: Flavin photoredox catalysis drives the Newman-Kwart rearrangement (phenol to thiophenol) under mild, aqueous-compatible conditions instead of heat. Supposedly more scalable, though substrate scope is a separate question.

original_title: Translation of a novel EphA2-targeted PET tracer from preclinical models to first-in-human studies in pancreatic cancer.
title: EphA2-targeted PET tracer reaches pancreatic cancer patients
blurb: A PET tracer targeting EphA2 (an Ephrin receptor) moving from preclinical studies into first-in-human trials in pancreatic cancer. Whether EphA2 imaging improves patient stratification or treatment selection over existing modalities is the real question.
venue: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

original_title: Precursor Engineering of Low-Coordinated Oxygen-Bearing Cu Electrocatalysts for Selective CO 2 Reduction toward Multicarbon Products
title: Engineered copper catalyst for CO₂ reduction to multicarbon products
blurb: Precursor engineering to build low-coordinated copper-oxygen sites for CO₂ electroreduction to multicarbon products like ethylene. Whether the selectivity and current efficiency beat existing catalysts is the question that actually matters.

original_title: Ultrasound-Assisted Polymorph Control in Reactive Crystallization of l -Glutamic Acid: Aiming for Consistently Producing Stable Form
title: Ultrasound crystallization reliably produces stable L-glutamic acid polymorph
blurb: Ultrasound-assisted reactive crystallization to selectively produce the thermodynamically stable polymorph of L-glutamic acid, avoiding metastable forms. Polymorph control is a real manufacturing problem, so a reliable sonication-based method could matter if it translates to scale.

original_title: Iridium-Catalyzed Decarboxylative Annulation of 2- o -Carboranylbenzoic Acids with Internal Alkynes
title: Iridium-catalyzed annulation fuses carboranes to alkynes
blurb: Iridium-catalyzed decarboxylative annulation coupling 2-o-carboranylbenzoic acids with internal alkynes to build carborane-fused ring systems. Carboranes appear in drug design, so routes to fused scaffolds could be useful, depending on substrate compatibility.

original_title: One-step cleavage and purification: a bifunctional IdeS-Protein A fusion protein for on-column generation of antibody fragments
title: IdeS-Protein A fusion does antibody fragment prep on-column
blurb: The fusion protein cleaves IgG while staying bound to the column via its Protein A domain, so fragments and Fc separate in one step. Fewer transfers and buffers, though you're now committed to using this specific reagent instead of the standard modular approach.

original_title: Exclusive Markovnikov Synthesis of Tertiary Phosphine Sulfides from Secondary Phosphine Oxides, S 8 , and Alkenes
title: Markovnikov addition of sulfur to phosphine oxides
blurb: Secondary phosphine oxides react with S₈ and alkenes to furnish tertiary phosphine sulfides with clean Markovnikov selectivity. It's an efficient route to a useful functional group, though substrate scope is the real test.

original_title: Visible-Light-Induced Hydrosilylation Enabled by an In Situ Assembled Photoactive Complex
title: Hydrosilylation driven by visible light
blurb: An in situ-assembled photoactive complex catalyzes hydrosilylation under visible light instead of thermal or UV conditions. Substituting thermal heating for light is the goal, and preliminary scope looks reasonable, though how it compares to existing hydrosilylation methods is the real question to answer.

original_title: Study of Carfilzomib, Lenalidomide, Dexamethasone and Belantamab Mafodotin in Multiple Myeloma
title: Belantamab mafodotin joins standard three-drug myeloma regimen
blurb: Phase 1/2 combination study adding belantamab mafodotin (BCMA-directed ADC) to carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone in multiple myeloma. Early phase and Wake Forest data, so the main question is tolerability, since four-drug combinations tend to pile up side effects.

original_title: Synthesis of thieno[2,3- b ]indoles by Cloke–Wilson rearrangement of spiro donor–acceptor cyclopropanes
title: Cloke-Wilson rearrangement gives thieno[2,3-b]indoles in one pot
blurb: Spiro donor-acceptor cyclopropyl oxindoles undergo Lawesson's-catalyzed thiocarbonyl formation, Cloke-Wilson rearrangement, and oxidative aromatization all in one pot. It's a solid route to a useful fused indole, though scope and generality beyond the paper's examples is the real test.

original_title: Unlocking ketene–Heck coupling via activation energy modulation in Pd catalysis
title: Heck-type coupling between acid chlorides and iodoarenes
blurb: Saturated acid chlorides couple with iodoarenes via Pd catalysis to give α,β-unsaturated ketones, going through a ketene intermediate. It's an expansion of Heck chemistry into a new substrate class, though how the scope stacks up against standard Heck reactions is the practical question.

original_title: Competency assessment of synthesized titanate nanowires for preconcentration of terbium from monazite liquors
title: Titanate nanowires extract terbium from ore solutions
blurb: Hydrothermal synthesis yields titanate nanowires that sorb Tb-160 from monazite liquors, with pH, temperature, and contact time all optimized. The nanowires are regenerable, though the practical advantage over existing rare-earth separation methods isn't clear from the abstract alone.

original_title: Imaging xCT with Immuno-PET in Therapy-Resistant Non–Small Cell Lung Cancer
title: PET imaging of xCT in therapy-resistant lung cancer
blurb: This immuno-PET study images the cystine-glutamate transporter (xCT) in therapy-resistant non-small cell lung cancer. The premise is likely that xCT expression marks resistance or drives it, though you'd need to see the clinical correlations to know if it's prognostic or just descriptive.

original_title: Chemoenzymatic Synthesis of Well‐Defined α(2,8)‐ and α(2,9)‐Linked Oligosialosides
title: Defined oligosialosides synthesis via recombinant polymerases
blurb: Recombinant bacterial sialic acid polymerases (NmC-PST and NmB-PST) synthesize α(2,8)- and α(2,9)-linked oligosialosides of defined lengths using protected CMP-Neu5Ac substrates. It's a clean enzymatic route to oligosaccharides that would be tedious to synthesize chemically, and the protective groups (benzoyl and thioethyl) let you control which linkage you build.

original_title: Palladium-Catalyzed Unsymmetrical bis-Allylation of Active Methylene Compounds Using Vinyl Sulfoxonium Ylides and Allyl Esters
title: Sulfoxonium ylides drive bis-allylation of active methylenes
blurb: Active methylene compounds undergo Pd-catalyzed bis-allylation with vinyl sulfoxonium ylides and allyl esters, incorporating two different allyl groups. Sulfoxonium ylides keep expanding their role in Pd catalysis, and introducing two different groups instead of symmetrical substitution is a real advance, though substrate scope will tell you if this generalizes.

original_title: Atomically Defined Photoswitchable Hybrid Nanostructures Built from Functionalized Norbornadienes and Cobalt Oxide Nanoislands on Au(111)
title: Norbornadienes that photoswitch even when anchored to metal
blurb: Most surface-bound photoswitches lose their responsiveness or show altered behavior on conductive surfaces, but these norbornadienes on cobalt oxide retain photoactivity closer to their solution-phase behavior. It's still a few steps from a practical device, but it's a start on the design problem.

original_title: Advancing Biochemical Molecule Registration, Representation and Search for New Drug Modalities
title: New drug modalities still don't have cheminformatics standards
blurb: Small molecules have mature databases and schemas, but therapeutic peptides, oligonucleotides, and ADCs don't fit those molds and lack alternatives. The result is that drug companies are managing multiple modalities without reliable digital systems to describe or exchange them.
venue: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling

original_title: Spin-controlled enantioselective near-infrared photocatalysis with chiral MgO/Co3O4 nanoparticles
title: Chiral nanoparticles that catalyze in the near-infrared
blurb: Cobalt oxide nanoparticles doped with magnesium and functionalized with chiral amino acids catalyze enantioselective hydroxylation of tyrosine to DOPA under NIR light. It's a single-substrate demonstration, and whether NIR excitation offers real advantages to justify the complexity versus room-temperature catalysis remains an open question.

original_title: Calcination-driven structure–activity relationship of SnO₂–ZrO₂ mixed oxide catalysts for highly selective transfer hydrogenation of cinnamaldehyde to cinnamyl alcohol
title: Calcination temperature tunes mixed-oxide catalyst activity
blurb: Tin and zirconium oxides mixed 1:1 show a clear correlation between calcination temperature, structural changes, and catalytic activity for transfer hydrogenation of cinnamaldehyde to cinnamyl alcohol. It's cheap and selective, but the substrate scope is narrow.

original_title: Photocatalytic, Scalable Anti-Markovnikov Hydrocarboxylation of Unactivated Alkenes via Amine-Mediated CO 2 •– Generation from Formate
title: Carboxylating unactivated alkenes with formate and light
blurb: Formate salt generates CO2•– under photocatalysis that adds across unactivated double bonds in anti-Markovnikov fashion, a reaction that pressurized CO2 hasn't really achieved. The title claims generality, but the incomplete snippet doesn't show whether the scope actually pans out.

original_title: Palladium-Catalyzed Meta-Selective Suzuki Coupling of 3,4-Dichloropyridine through N -Oxidation/Deoxygenation
title: N-oxide activation enables meta-selective pyridine coupling
blurb: Palladium-catalyzed Suzuki coupling on 3,4-dichloropyridine gets directed to the meta position via N-oxide formation instead of the usual ortho or para. The available snippet provides no substrate scope data, so it's impossible to judge whether this is a broadly useful method or a one-off for this particular pyridine.

original_title: Photocatalytic halogen atom transfer enables general dicarbofunctionalization of alkenes with organic halides and CO2
title: Dicarbofunctionalization of alkenes via photocatalytic HAT
blurb: Photocatalytic halogen atom transfer merges organic halides and CO2 across double bonds in a single pot. The title promises generality, but without substrate scope or selectivity data, it's too early to know if it delivers.

original_title: π‐Extended Salphen Scaffolds Enable CO 2 Electroreduction and Singlet Oxygen Generation
title: Extended salphen ligands for CO2 reduction and photooxygenation
blurb: π-Extended salen ligands with phenylene bridges show tunable redox activity and photophysical properties suitable for both electrocatalytic CO2 reduction and singlet oxygen generation. The snippet cuts off before showing actual performance, so it's unclear whether these beat the existing toolkit or just offer another tunable alternative.

original_title: Animal Tumor Models for Boron Neutron Capture Therapy Studies (Excluding Central Nervous System Solid Tumors)
title: Animal models for boron neutron capture therapy research
blurb: A survey of in vivo radiobiological studies across multiple boron compounds, administration routes, and dosing strategies in various tumor models. It's a useful translational reference, though animal models represent the beginning of the clinical story rather than the finish line.

original_title: Unlocking the activities of polyglutamylation enzymes in brain tubulin with top-down mass spectrometry
title: Mapping tubulin glutamylation with top-down mass spec
blurb: Top-down mass spectrometry resolves the full landscape of polyglutamylated forms on tubulin's C-terminal tail, which regulates how microtubule-binding proteins interact with the cytoskeleton. It's a measurement tool rather than new biology, but the resolution should clarify how glutamylation patterns control tubulin function.

original_title: Direct conversion of N2 and CH4 to valuable N-containing compounds via a plasma-activated catalytic process
title: Nitrogen and methane to N-compounds via plasma catalysis
blurb: A plasma-activated catalytic process converts N2 and CH4 directly to N-containing products without intermediate steps. The substrate scope and selectivity remain unclear, so it's too early to judge whether this scales beyond the lab.

original_title: Chemo selective synthesis and antibacterial assessment of pyrazolo[3,4-d]pyrimidinones via a novel urea/thiourea-based ternary DES
title: A deep eutectic solvent that acts as its own catalyst
blurb: A three-component mixture of ZnCl2, choline chloride, and thiourea functions as solvent, reagent, and catalyst for pyrazolo compound synthesis. The products show antibacterial activity, though whether this method beats conventional synthesis depends on the cost and reusability of the solvent.

original_title: Strain‐Release Pentafluorosulfanylation of Carbonyl‐Containing Disubstituted Bicyclobutanes: A Fortuitous Path to SF 5 ‐Containing Oxa[2.1.1]bicyclohexanes
title: Adding the SF5 group through ring strain release
blurb: Pentafluorosulfanylation of strained bicyclobutanes uses strain-release and radical polarity matching to control regioselectivity. The SF5 toolkit keeps expanding, though this is mainly a synthetic tool rather than solving a specific bottleneck.

original_title: High-throughput mutational analysis of F(1)-ATPase by integrated cell-free protein synthesis and single-molecule rotation assay.
title: Screening F1-ATPase variants via single-molecule rotation
blurb: Cell-free protein synthesis combined with single-molecule rotation assays enables mutational analysis of F1-ATPase in one integrated workflow. It's a useful platform if the throughput and data quality hold across hundreds of variants, which the abstract doesn't confirm.

original_title: Interfacial Active Sites in Catalysts for Higher Alcohol Synthesis From CO 2 : A Review
title: Making higher alcohols from CO2, if thermodynamics cooperates
blurb: A review of catalysts for CO2 hydrogenation to higher alcohols, focusing on interfacial active sites for controlling selectivity. The real barrier is thermodynamic—methane and methanol are favored—so catalyst design alone won't crack it, though recent work on specific interfacial geometries offers a partial workaround.

original_title: Lithium Diisopropylamide Mediated Synthesis of 3-Amino-1,2-Disubstituted Indoles via Intramolecular Cyclization of Amides
title: 3-Amino indoles from LDA-catalyzed cyclization
blurb: Lithium diisopropylamide promotes intramolecular cyclization of amides to build 3-amino indoles directly. Substrate scope and functional-group tolerance are not given in the abstract, so it's unclear how broadly this applies.

original_title: Blue Light Irradiated, Para Site-Selective C–H/C–H Cross-Dehydrogenative Coupling of Electron-Rich Arenes with Quinones
title: Blue light coupling arenes to quinones, para-selective
blurb: Blue light drives C–H/C–H cross-dehydrogenative coupling between electron-rich arenes and quinones with para selectivity favored. Photochemistry is fashionable, but thermal methods already achieve this regioselectivity, so the advantage of needing illumination isn't obvious.

original_title: Modular in vitro evaluation of buparlisib-polymeric nanomedicines in 2D and 3D models of glioblastoma
title: Testing PI3K inhibitor polymers in 3D tumor spheroids
blurb: A modular platform integrates polymer–drug conjugation with stepwise cytotoxicity screening in both 2D and 3D glioblastoma models using buparlisib as a test agent. Whether 3D geometry actually predicts in vivo outcomes better than 2D monolayers is still speculative.

original_title: Green supported of palladium nanoparticles over the CNT/Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticles: A recyclable catalyst for Buchwald–Hartwig C–N cross coupling reaction and study of its anti-breast cancer properties
title: Magnetically recoverable Pd catalyst for C–N coupling
blurb: Palladium nanoparticles anchored to magnetic carbon nanotubes enable Buchwald–Hartwig C–N coupling with straightforward recovery and reuse via magnetism. The paper also screened the products against breast cancer cells, but that's a separate finding from the catalyst innovation.

original_title: [ 124 I]I-cobimetinib PET enables noninvasive tracking of MEK inhibition in MAPK-driven cancers
title: A PET tracer tracking MEK inhibitor target engagement
blurb: Copper-mediated radioiodination of a boron pinacol precursor yields [124I]I-cobimetinib with high radiochemical yield and specific activity for PET imaging. It's useful for mapping MAPK-driven tumors, though you'll need additional pharmacokinetic work to separate true target engagement from simple accumulation.

original_title: Synthesis of 1,3-Disubstituted 3-Azabicyclo[3.2.0]heptane Libraries for Fragment-Based Drug Discovery
title: A library of azabicyclic scaffolds for drug discovery
blurb: Organic synthesis of 1,3-disubstituted 3-azabicyclo[3.2.0]heptanes as fragment-based drug discovery scaffolds. The compounds enable FBDD campaigns, though whether these fragments become actual hits is where the real work starts.

original_title: Iodide-Promoted Oxidative S-Arylation of Sulfenamides with Phenols for the Synthesis of Sulfilimines
title: S-Arylating sulfenamides with phenols and iodide
blurb: An oxidative S-arylation of sulfenamides using phenols as the aryl source and iodide as promoter, making sulfilimines. The reaction works as described, though substrate scope determines whether this becomes a standard transformation.

original_title: Four-Step Continuous-Flow Total Synthesis of 4-Chloro-6,7-dimethoxyquinoline, a Key Intermediate for Tyrosine Kinase Inhibitors
title: A four-step flow synthesis of a TKI intermediate
blurb: Continuous-flow synthesis of 4-chloro-6,7-dimethoxyquinoline, a known precursor for tyrosine kinase inhibitors, compressed to four steps. It's useful if your TKI program needs kilogram quantities on a reasonable timeline.

original_title: Highly Efficient Isolation and Rapid Detection of Pancreatic Cancer-Derived Small Extracellular Vesicles by Specific Aggregation from Aptamer-Functionalized DNA Long Chains
title: Isolating pancreatic cancer EVs with aptamer-functionalized DNA
blurb: A method for rapidly isolating and detecting pancreatic cancer-derived small extracellular vesicles using aptamer-functionalized DNA long chains. Aptamer targeting is well-established, so the payoff is whether the enrichment is high enough in real samples.

original_title: Boron Amidate as a Modular Anionic Catalyst for Sequential Electron-Donor/Hydrogen-Atom-Transfer Catalysis
title: A boron amidate catalyst for coupled electron and hydrogen transfer
blurb: A modular anionic boron amidate that combines EDA complex formation with hydrogen atom transfer catalysis, skipping external photocatalysts. The radical chemistry should work in principle, though substrate scope determines whether it's a general toolkit.

original_title: Optimization of a Resolution Process Allowing Access to Both Enantiomers of the Versatile Chiral Phosphine Ligand sSPhos on Scale
title: Scaling both enantiomers of sSPhos
blurb: An optimized resolution process for sSPhos, a widely used chiral phosphine ligand, that works on scale for both enantiomers. It's useful if your project needs enantiomeric quantities that current suppliers don't stock.

original_title: Real-time electrical monitoring of enzymatic catalytic dynamics at the single-molecule level.
title: Electrical detection of single-molecule enzyme catalysis
blurb: Real-time electrical monitoring of enzyme turnover at the single-molecule level. It's a useful sensor if you're studying individual catalytic events, though most biochemical questions don't require this resolution.

original_title: Topological Control of Paratropicity via Alternative Ring Fusion in Indacenodithiophene Isomers
title: Ring fusion controls aromaticity in indacenodithiophenes
blurb: Manipulating ring fusion in indacenodithiophene isomers controls paratropicity in the conjugated core. It's a topology study of how skeletal structure governs electronic properties rather than a synthesis of new compounds.

original_title: The Mechanism of Visible‐Light Photochemical Rearrangements of Conjugated Amide Enolates
title: Visible-light rearrangements of conjugated amide enolates
blurb: Photochemistry of lithium enolates from conjugated amides undergoes skeletal rearrangements and ring expansions via a biradical intermediate. The mechanism is clear, but translating this into a practical synthesis depends on controlling the selectivity.

original_title: Recent advances in the organocatalytic enantioselective construction of alkynyl-containing tetrasubstituted carbon stereocenters
title: Building quaternary stereocenters with alkynes through organocatalysis
blurb: A review of recent organocatalytic methods for making tetrasubstituted stereocenters bearing an alkyne group. Reviews are useful snapshots of solved problems, though the frontier is always in the newest papers.

original_title: Radnostix Acquires Lucerno Dynamics Assets Expanding Radiopharma Medical Device Portfolio - TradingView
title: Radnostix acquires Lucerno's radiopharma devices
blurb: Radnostix expanding its radiopharma line by acquiring Lucerno Dynamics. The announcement provides no detail on what Lucerno built or why it's strategically important.

original_title: Rapid Access to Photoswitchable RNA Binders: Fluorination Enhances Protein Rescue by Exon Inclusion
title: Photoswitchable RNA binders, now fluorinated
blurb: Fluorinated photo-switchable small molecules that target RNA and drive exon inclusion as a protein rescue mechanism. Light gating lets you control when these transient-binding molecules engage, which is hard to achieve otherwise. Still a chemical proof of concept, though.

original_title: PSMAddition Trial of ADT, ARPI, and Lutetium-177 in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer - Fred Saad - UroToday
title: Adding Lu-177 to hormone therapy in early metastatic prostate cancer
blurb: The PSMAddition trial combining lutetium-177 PSMA with standard ADT and ARPIs in newly metastatic disease. Early intensification with radiopharmaceutical and hormonal pressure together is reasonable in principle, but showing benefit at this stage is a high bar.

original_title: Controlled ROS generation in pyridinium-functionalized nanocatalysts for selective photooxidation
title: Pyridinium nanocatalysts for selective alcohol oxidation
blurb: Ultrasonic fragmentation of a coordination polymer yields nanoparticles that photocatalyze selective oxidation of alcohols, with water regulating ROS formation to favor singlet oxygen. Selective photocatalysis is genuinely hard. Whether this scales from the lab is another question.

original_title: High-throughput small-angle X-ray scattering reveals effective structure factor transitions linked to high-concentration antibody viscosity
title: Antibodies show structural transitions at high concentration
blurb: High-throughput SAXS revealing how antibodies transition structurally as they concentrate, correlating with the viscosity spike that makes concentrated mAb formulations annoying. Mostly a methods result, but useful if you're scaling production or designing antibody cocktails.

original_title: Engineering BN‐Doped Open‐Shell Polycyclic Hydrocarbons for Enhanced Photothermal Conversion and Therapy
title: BN-doped organic photothermal agents
blurb: A class of atomically precise boron-nitrogen-doped polycyclic hydrocarbons offering improved photothermal conversion and biodegradability compared to inorganic or undoped carbon agents. Organic PTAs usually trade efficiency for lower toxicity, so the BN doping addresses a gap. Whether they actually work in tissue is the catch.

original_title: Cyclopropylamine‐Derived Distonic Iminium Radicals: Photoinduced Strategies in Chemo‐ and Stereo‐Selective Synthesis
title: Cyclopropylamines as radical precursors in photocatalysis
blurb: Photocatalytic ring-opening and cycloadditions built on cyclopropylamine strain release and distonic iminium radical chemistry. The ring strain makes the reactivity work, but this is more a synthetic toolkit than a solution to anything in particular downstream.

original_title: Proteoform-specific enrichment of phosphopeptide isomers by polymer-based synthetic receptors
title: Polymer receptors sort phosphopeptide isomers
blurb: Synthetic polymer-based receptors that resolve phosphopeptide positional isomers and pull ultralow-abundance species from complex human proteomes. Phosphorylation site mapping is a real analytics bottleneck, and high-resolution enrichment is hard. Still a maybe until someone runs it on their actual protein.

original_title: Electronic effects on the stability of heteroleptic nickel(II) complexes with aromatic and aliphatic ligands
title: Tuning nickel complex stability with mixed ligands
blurb: Heteroleptic nickel(II) complexes combining xanthate and nitrogen-donor ligands, with electronic properties tuned by the donor mix. Xanthates are versatile sulfur ligands that influence stability and electronic structure. Useful if you're designing Ni catalysts, but mostly a ligand-optimization study.

original_title: Soft Segment Engineering of Bio-Based Poly(propylene 2,5-furandicarboxylate) for Enhanced Toughness and Spinnability
title: Toughening furan polyesters with soft segments
blurb: A furan-based polyester engineered for higher toughness and spinnability by incorporating polyether glycol as a soft segment. Furan polyesters are attractive biobased alternatives to petroleum plastics but tend to be brittle and slow-crystallizing. Whether the mechanical properties hold up in production is the real question.
venue: Journal of Polymers and the Environment

original_title: Ligand directed nanocarriers enable brain selective neuroprotection through optimized blood brain barrier transport and translational design
title: Ligand-directed nanocarriers to cross the blood-brain barrier
blurb: The BBB blocks most drugs from reaching the brain, and ligand-coated nanocarriers are meant to get past it with selective homing. This mini-review covers the mechanistic rationale, though clinical track record for these systems is still thin.

original_title: Injectable hydrogel scaffolds with sustained bioactive release and multilevel architecture for cartilage repair.
title: Injectable hydrogels with sustained drug release and cartilage support
blurb: Hydrogel scaffolds with multilevel architecture designed for sustained bioactive release while supporting cartilage regeneration. The materials engineering makes sense in principle, though whether this complexity translates to better outcomes in actual cartilage repair remains to be shown.

original_title: A Practical and Scalable-Oriented Enantioselective Synthesis of ( S )-Autumnaline
title: A practical and scalable synthesis of (S)-autumnaline
blurb: Enantioselective synthesis of the alkaloid autumnaline explicitly engineered for production scale rather than gram-scale laboratory prep. Publication in Organic Process Research & Development suggests the route actually works, not just in a flask.

original_title: Assessing collagenase-a and type-II mediated degradation of human type-I collagen by photoacoustic spectroscopy toward clinical translation in cancer progression
title: A photoacoustic sensor for collagen degradation in tumors
blurb: Compact photoacoustic spectroscopy catching collagen breakdown in real time without labels, aimed at tracking tumor-driven matrix remodeling as a sign of cancer progression. Still preclinical, but the optical approach is sound if the signal penetrates tissue well enough to be useful.

original_title: Development and Preclinical Evaluation of (68)Ga-Labeled B7-H3-Specific Bicyclic Peptides as Immuno-PET Tracers for Oncology.
title: Gallium-68 peptides targeting B7-H3 for cancer PET imaging
blurb: Bicyclic peptides labeled with Ga-68 designed to image B7-H3, an immune checkpoint protein present in various cancers. Still in preclinical evaluation, so in vivo selectivity and whether B7-H3 imaging actually tracks clinically relevant disease remain to be shown.

original_title: Germanium ligand redox cooperativity: A key to ambiphilicity and switchable two- and four-electron transfer
title: Germanium ligands that do both two- and four-electron chemistry
blurb: Germanium-based ligands with redox cooperativity that enables them to participate in either two-electron or four-electron transfers depending on substrate and conditions. The redox flexibility is mechanistically interesting, though practical synthetic applications aren't obvious yet.

original_title: A Ce 4+ Aluminum Hydride Complex
title: A stable cerium(IV)-aluminum hydride complex
blurb: Ce in the +4 oxidation state in a stable complex is uncommon outside of high-valent transient species, and this one carries an aluminum hydride ligand. The chemistry clears the bar for JACS, though whether the compound does anything useful beyond existing is unclear.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Oscillating Potential Engineering to Overcome Scaling Relationship Constraints in Alcohol Selective Oxidation
title: Oscillating voltage to dodge scaling limits in alcohol oxidation
blurb: Electrochemical catalysis for selective alcohol oxidation usually hits a scaling trap where improving one rate constant damages another, and periodic potential pulses are meant to sidestep that. Whether the approach avoids electrode degradation and catalyst poisoning at useful current density is the question that matters.

original_title: Constructing dense three-dimensional networks in collagen/chitosan films via chemical cross-linking and metal ion coordination for improved water resistance and strength.
title: Collagen-chitosan films cross-linked with metal coordination
blurb: Collagen and chitosan films strengthened with both chemical cross-links and metal ion coordination to improve water resistance and mechanical strength. The multilevel approach is reasonable materials engineering, though whether all three components are equally necessary isn't clear from the title.

original_title: AdvanCell locks in lease for US headquarters, radiopharmaceutical production near Boston - Fierce Pharma
title: AdvanCell setting up radiopharmaceutical production near Boston
blurb: The company locked in a facility lease for US headquarters and manufacturing, betting on the radiopharmaceutical space. Real estate commitments suggest commercial confidence, though they don't guarantee the products will actually work or reach patients.

blurb: A cascade cyclization and oxidative annulation using Cu(II) to make the tricyclic core in one pot. Substrate scope and yields aren't in the snippet, so it's hard to judge whether this changes synthesis practice.

title: Tracking a biomimetic complex's photodynamics
blurb: Spectroscopy watching what a lumichrome-amine complex does under light across femtoseconds through milliseconds. It's a methods demonstration, but whether the actual findings matter isn't clear from the abstract.

title: Plasma and copper oxides convert CO2 to methanol
blurb: Non-thermal plasma catalyzes CO2 hydrogenation with help from CuxO and MgO under mild conditions. Plasma efficiency at scale has always been the bottleneck though, so that's the real test.

title: Fluorinated quantum dots detect pyrethroid pesticides

title: A deep-UV nonlinear optical crystal outperforms KBBF
blurb: A crystal with unconventional coordination geometry hits record deep-UV performance while sidestepping KBBF's toxicity and growth difficulty. Whether it actually grows clean at scale is the open question.

original_title: Photocyclization of 3DPA2FBN Yielding Core-Edge Fused Donor-Acceptor Cyanoarene Photocatalysts
title: Blue light turns a commercial cyanoarene into a photocatalyst
blurb: 3DPA2FBN undergoes photocyclization under blue light at room temperature, losing HF and fusing to a donor-acceptor scaffold. Stability in use is where these photocatalysts usually fail, so that's what matters.

title: S-layer proteins for engineered living materials
blurb: S-layer proteins form 2D lattices and can be engineered into self-assembling biomaterials that regenerate themselves. Still conceptual work, but the self-repair mechanism is the core idea worth pursuing.
